mnemonics: CPU ALU PC MAR MDR - Cows Always Play Music, Making Dull Rhythms (Control Unit, ALU, Program Counter, Memory Address Register, Memory Data Register)
MAR Addresses, MDR Stores Data - M.A.R.S. & M.D.S.
Input, Output, Memory, ALU, Control - I Only Make Absolute Control
keyPoints: The CPU is the brain, containing the Control Unit (orchestrator) and ALU (calculator).
Memory stores instructions (program) and data. MAR holds the address, MDR holds the data.
The system bus (address, data, control) is the communication highway connecting all components.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Memory Address Register (MAR) with the Memory Data Register (MDR). MAR says WHERE, MDR says WHAT.
Forgetting the Control Unit's role, thinking the ALU runs the show. The ALU just calculates; the Control Unit directs traffic.
Thinking the data bus is bidirectional (data can flow both ways) while the address bus is unidirectional (CPU only outputs addresses).
